title: Early post–CAR-T relapse of follicular lymphoma grade 1–2 in a 55-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: Fever, night sweats, weight loss, abdominal pain, and dyspnea in a patient with prior follicular lymphoma and CAR-T therapy
patient.vital_signs: Temperature 39.5 °C, blood pressure 105/65 mmHg, heart rate 132/min (irregularly irregular), respiratory rate 22/min, oxygen saturation 91% on room air.

1.capabilities: You are assessing a systemically unwell patient with prior lymphoma and recent cellular therapy who presents with fever, B-symptoms, and signs of organ involvement. You can take a detailed history, perform a focused examination, and arrange general blood tests and cross-sectional imaging appropriate to an urgent cancer assessment. Initial decisions focus on stabilizing the patient, clarifying the need for admission, and identifying major complications such as organ compromise.

2.setting: Inpatient lymphoma workup (staging and organ assessment)
2.capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further workup. You can obtain tissue samples from accessible sites, perform more detailed staging and organ assessment, and involve other specialties for management of organ compromise. Results from broader imaging and laboratory evaluations can be integrated, while some specialized tests may still be pending.

3.setting: Specialist lymphoma and cellular therapy conference
3.capabilities: You now have histology and molecular results from involved organs together with full staging and a detailed treatment history including prior chemoimmunotherapy, autologous transplantation, bispecific antibodies, and CAR-T therapy. You are expected to establish the lymphoma subtype, risk category, and a realistic systemic treatment plan that considers comorbidities, prior exposure, and the patient’s reluctance for prolonged inpatient stays.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for age, sex, comorbidities, social situation, family background, or anthropometrics.
demographics.content:
Age: 55
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 1
Comorbidities: Tachycardic atrial fibrillation with spontaneous conversion (CHA₂DS₂-VASc score = 2). Coronary two-vessel disease with preserved systolic LV function. Proximal RCA stenosis hemodynamically not relevant. NSTEMI on 2023-10-03, followed by PTCA with DES stenting of a high-grade stenosis in the large D1 branch with proximal and mid LAD involvement. History of testicular tumor (surgery and radiotherapy, 2004). TUR-P for BPH (2016). Arterial hypertension. Hypercholesterolemia.
Social history: Married, 2 children (ages 15 and 18).
Height: 173 cm; Bodyweight: 85 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, B-symptoms, physical examination findings, or current medications.
history_physical.content:
2024-11-12: The patient presented with fever up to 40 °C, recurrent night sweats, unintentional weight loss of approximately 5 kg within six weeks, and diffuse abdominal pain without peritoneal signs. He additionally reported exertional dyspnea corresponding to NYHA class II–III and described a single episode of syncope without trauma. He expressed reluctance toward further inpatient treatment due to family and business obligations.
Current medication: apixaban 5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 40 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, magnesium supplement once daily, and paracetamol or metamizole as needed for fever or pain.
Vital signs: temperature 39.5 °C, blood pressure 105/65 mmHg, heart rate 132/min (irregularly irregular), respiratory rate 22/min, oxygen saturation 91% on room air.

cbc_chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count, differential, coagulation profile, chemistry, and cardiac markers.
cbc_chemistry.content:
Hb 10.8 g/dL, WBC 24.2 × 10⁹/L, platelets 93 × 10⁹/L; MCV 91 fL, MCH 29 pg; differential: neutrophils 18%, lymphocytes 72%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: aPTT 32 sec, INR 2.1.
Chemistry: Na 133 mmol/L, K 5.8 mmol/L, creatinine 2.4 mg/dL, BUN 52 mg/dL, AST 68 U/L, ALT 74 U/L, bilirubin 2.2 mg/dL, LDH 1180 U/L, uric acid 9.6 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.10 mmol/L, CRP 18.6 mg/dL, procalcitonin 2.2 ng/mL.
Cardiac markers: NT-proBNP 2450 pg/mL, troponin T 0.034 ng/mL.

ultrasound_abdomen_echo.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or echocardiography findings.
ultrasound_abdomen_echo.content:
2024-11-12: Abdominal ultrasound showed large confluent lymph node masses, predominantly along the left ureter, mesenteric and retroperitoneal regions, and the hepatic hilum. There was associated left-sided hydronephrosis grade IV and hepatosplenomegaly (spleen 16.4 × 8.1 cm) with moderate 4-quadrant ascites. The bladder wall was mildly thickened, the gallbladder showed mild wall edema, and the common bile duct was dilated to 1.0 cm.
Echocardiography demonstrated a mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction of 47% and a large circumferential pericardial effusion without clear signs of tamponade.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow aspirate, biopsy, and immunophenotyping findings.
bone_marrow.content:
Bone marrow cytology: The bone marrow was normocellular with preserved trilineage hematopoiesis. There was an increased interstitial and paratrabecular infiltration (approximately 30%) by small- to medium-sized lymphoid cells with irregular, condensed chromatin and scant, pale cytoplasm. No blast excess was identified. Megakaryopoiesis was reduced in number but morphologically preserved. Erythroid and myeloid maturation were intact.
Flow cytometry: An expanded B-cell population was detected, expressing CD19, CD10, CD22, and weak CD20, with FMC7 dim positivity. The cells were negative for CD5, CD23, CD11c, and CD103, and demonstrated kappa light-chain restriction. Myeloid progenitors showed a normal distribution. T-cells displayed an inverted CD4/CD8 and the NK-cell fraction was normal. No increase in CD34⁺ blasts or aberrant myeloid populations was seen.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional karyotype or cytogenetic overview.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for infection workup, immunologic status, PET-CT results, and urologic assessment.
extended_diagnostics.content:
Urinalysis revealed marked leukocyturia (>500/µL), a positive nitrite test, and bacteria (+++). Urine culture grew Escherichia coli (no ESBL). Urology consultation recommended DJ stent placement to relieve left-sided urinary obstruction secondary to retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
Viral serology was negative for hepatitis B (HBsAg negative, anti-HBc negative), hepatitis C (anti-HCV negative) and HIV (HIV-1/2 Ab/Ag negative). CMV and EBV serologies were consistent with past infection (CMV IgG positive, IgM negative; EBV VCA IgG positive, IgM negative).
Immunologic evaluation showed a CD4⁺ T-cell count of 420/µL, CD8⁺ T-cell count 520/µL (CD4/CD8 ratio 0.8). Immunoglobulin levels were within normal limits (IgG 950 mg/dL).
FDG-PET/CT demonstrated intensely FDG-avid (Deauville 5) nodal and extranodal lymphoma manifestations in the right supradiaphragmatic, axillary, and mediastinal regions, as well as in the left retroperitoneum with encasement of the ureter, causing hydronephrosis grade IV, and multifocal hepatic involvement (segments IV, V, VI–VIII).
Radiology consultation advised percutaneous liver biopsy to further characterize FDG-avid hepatic lesions. Oral anticoagulation with apixaban was temporarily paused in preparation for the procedure. Paracentesis was performed on November 13, 2024, with drainage of 900 mL.

liver_biopsy.valid_request_if: Requests for histology, immunohistochemistry, and FISH results from hepatic lesions.
liver_biopsy.content:
Ultrasound-guided liver biopsy showed diffuse infiltration by a predominantly monomorphic lymphoid population composed of small cells with dense chromatin and narrow, pale cytoplasm. No blastoid morphology was observed. The infiltration extended into adipose tissue with partial effacement of the normal architecture. Focal sclerotic bands were present. Immunohistochemistry demonstrated a CD19-positive B-cell infiltrate co-expressing CD10 and BCL2, negative for CD5, with weak CD20 expression. Numerous admixed reactive T-lymphocytes expressing CD3 and CD5 were noted. The proliferative activity (Ki-67/MIB1) was irregular, focally up to 25–30%, with an average of approximately 15%.
FISH demonstrated t(14;18)(q32;q21) and 17p/TP53 deletion. No MYC or BCL6 rearrangements were detected.

pericardial_cytology.valid_request_if: Requests for pericardial fluid cytology and immunophenotype.
pericardial_cytology.content: Pericardial effusion cytospin preparations showed a low-cellularity specimen composed predominantly of reactive mesothelial cells and numerous macrophages. Scattered small- to medium-sized lymphoid cells formed a minor component of the cellular population. B-lymphocytes expressed CD19 and CD10 with surface kappa light-chain restriction and dim CD20 expression, negative for CD5, CD23, and CD200.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a statement on measurable residual disease or markers for response assessment.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior lymphoma treatments, including chemoimmunotherapy, autologous transplantation, bispecific antibodies, and CAR-T therapy.
treatment_history.content:
03/12/2020 – Initial diagnosis: Follicular lymphoma grade I–II, Ann Arbor stage IV. Diagnosis confirmed by biopsy performed during work-up for suspected testicular cancer relapse. Sites of involvement: Mediastinal, bilateral hilar, peribronchial (left upper lobe), coeliac, mesenteric, and retroperitoneal lymph nodes with bilateral renal hilum infiltration and encasement of the inferior vena cava, left inguinal nodes, axial skeleton, proximal long bones, and prostate. Bone marrow biopsy revealed low-level infiltration (5%).
First-line therapy (03/24/2020): Six cycles of O-CHOP (obinutuzumab 100 mg day 1 + 900 mg day 2 [cycle 1], 1000 mg day 1 [cycles 2–6]; cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m² day 1; doxorubicin 50 mg/m² day 1; vincristine 1.4 mg/m² [max 2 mg] day 1; prednisone 100 mg days 1–5). After the first cycle, the patient developed laboratory tumor lysis syndrome (uric acid 10.8 mg/dL, K⁺ 5.9 mmol/L, LDH 1350 U/L) requiring hospitalization for i.v. hydration and rasburicase. Interim CT (06/03/2020): Partial remission (60% lymph node reduction).
End-of-treatment CT (08/26/2020): Good radiographic response, no residual bulky disease. PET-CT not performed.
Maintenance therapy: Obinutuzumab 1000 mg q8w; three doses administered (09/2020–02/2021).
02/18/2021: Disease progression with mesenteric and retroperitoneal nodal enlargement; biopsy confirmed relapsed follicular lymphoma grade I–II, without transformation.
02/25/2021: R-DHAP initiated (rituximab 375 mg/m² day 1; dexamethasone 40 mg days 1–4; cisplatin 100 mg/m² day 1; cytarabine 2 g/m² q12h × 2 doses day 2). Apheresis: After cycle 2 (6.2 x 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg). Complications: Grade 4 neutropenia with febrile episode during cycle 2, treated with meropenem 1 g q8h × 7 days. Response: Metabolic CR by PET-CT (05/05/2021).
Autologous HSCT (05/20/2021): Conditioning with BEAM regimen (BCNU 300 mg/m² day −7; etoposide 200 mg/m² days −6 to −3; cytarabine 200 mg/m² q12h days −6 to −3; melphalan 140 mg/m² day −2). Stem cell infusion on 05/27/2021. Engraftment achieved on day +11 (neutrophils >0.5 × 10⁹/L) and day +13 (platelets >20 × 10⁹/L). Maintenance: Rituximab 375 mg/m² q8w initiated post-transplant.
05/02/2022: Relapse with B-symptoms; PET-CT showed recurrent FDG-avid lymphadenopathy (retroperitoneal, mediastinal). Biopsy confirmed follicular lymphoma without transformation or MYC/BCL6 rearrangements.
06/01/2022: Enrolled in clinical trial with mosunetuzumab (step-up dosing: 1 mg day 1, 2 mg day 8, 60 mg day 15; cycle 2: 60 mg day 1; cycle 3 and beyond: 30 mg q21d). Response: Partial remission by CT after 3 cycles (08/2022). Toxicity: Grade 1 cytokine release syndrome (fever 38.5 °C, no vasopressor requirement), treated symptomatically. A total of 8 cycles were applied.
01/15/2024: Progressive disease with bulky abdominal nodes; PET-CT Deauville 5. Biopsy: Follicular lymphoma grade I–II, no transformation, MYC/BCL6 negative, no blastoid morphology.
Bridging therapy: Epcoritamab step-up (0.16 mg day 1, 0.8 mg day 8, 48 mg days 15 and 22) following T-cell apheresis (01/31/2024). Second cycle continued with 48 mg weekly.
CAR-T therapy: Tisagenlecleucel infused 04/27/2024 after lymphodepletion with fludarabine 30 mg/m²/day and cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m²/day (days −5 to −3). Toxicities: Grade 3 cytokine release syndrome (fever, hypotension responsive to fluids + noradrenaline), treated with tocilizumab ×4 and dexamethasone 10 mg BID ×2 days. No neurotoxicity observed. Response: PET-CT 05/29/2024 showed complete metabolic remission.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current relapse characterization.
disease_status.content: Early post–CAR-T relapse of follicular lymphoma grade I–II, without histologic transformation.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual information relevant to treatment decisions, including organ involvement, comorbidities, and patient preferences.
special_considerations.content: 11/2024: The patient now presents with systemic relapse of follicular lymphoma, manifesting as bulky intra-abdominal disease with grade IV left-sided hydronephrosis and pericardial involvement. Bone marrow shows low-grade infiltration without evidence of transformation. Performance status remains preserved (ECOG 1), but the patient reports strong reluctance to undergo another prolonged inpatient admission due to family obligations, including caring for two children and supporting his partner. Patient declines allogeneic stem cell transplantation. Emotional strain at the time of progression was considerable, requiring support from the psycho-oncology service. Current management must integrate close renal monitoring in the setting of obstructive uropathy and prior infections, and serial cardiac assessments due to pericardial involvement and prior chemotherapy exposure.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Lymphoma with high tumor burden, tumor lysis syndrome, and infection of unclear origin
differentials: Aggressive lymphoma or follicular lymphoma transformation

treatment_plan.action: Admit the patient for monitoring and stabilization, with attention to hemodynamics, respiratory status, and pericardial effusion.
Initiate empiric anti-infective therapy while obtaining cultures and further diagnostic workup, given fever, elevated inflammatory markers, and urinary findings.
Arrange urgent cross-sectional imaging and plan tissue biopsy to confirm or exclude lymphoma relapse, pausing anticoagulation beforehand.
Manage tumor lysis syndrome with need-based fluid therapy, furosemide, and rasburicase given elevated LDH, uric acid, and potassium.
Coordinate urologic intervention to relieve left-sided urinary obstruction and protect renal function.
Monitor the pericardial effusion closely with repeat assessments and involve cardiology to determine the need and timing of pericardial drainage.

key_findings_quotes: The patient presented with fever up to 40 C, recurrent night sweats, unintentional weight loss of approximately 5 kg within six weeks, and diffuse abdominal pain without peritoneal signs.
Abdominal ultrasound showed large confluent lymph node masses, predominantly along the left ureter, mesenteric and retroperitoneal regions, and the hepatic hilum.
There was associated left-sided hydronephrosis grade IV and hepatosplenomegaly (spleen 16.4 x 8.1 cm) with moderate 4-quadrant ascites.
Echocardiography demonstrated a mildly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction of 47% and a large circumferential pericardial effusion without clear signs of tamponade.
LDH 1180 U/L, uric acid 9.6 mg/dL, K 5.8 mmol/L, creatinine 2.4 mg/dL, CRP 18.6 mg/dL, procalcitonin 2.2 ng/mL.
He expressed reluctance toward further inpatient treatment due to family and business obligations.

treatment_plan.action: Integrate bone marrow flow cytometry and PET-CT findings to establish systemic relapse of a CD10-positive, kappa-restricted B-cell lymphoma.
Proceed with image-guided liver biopsy to confirm histology, assess proliferation, and evaluate high-risk features; pause anticoagulation before procedure.
Implement urologic decompression with DJ stent placement to address grade IV hydronephrosis and preserve kidney function.
Consider prednisolone prephase treatment (e.g., 100 mg daily for five days) under close monitoring for potential tumor lysis given extensive lymphadenopathy with ureteral compression.
Continue close cardiology follow-up for the pericardial effusion and consider drainage if clinical or echocardiographic signs of compromise appear.
Maintain symptomatic treatment for ascites and abdominal discomfort, including paracentesis as needed.

key_findings_quotes: An expanded B-cell population was detected, expressing CD19, CD10, CD22, and weak CD20, with FMC7 dim positivity.
The cells were negative for CD5, CD23, CD11c, and CD103, and demonstrated kappa light-chain restriction.

FDG-PET/CT demonstrated intensely FDG-avid (Deauville 5) nodal and extranodal lymphoma manifestations in the right supradiaphragmatic, axillary, and mediastinal regions, as well as in the left retroperitoneum with encasement of the ureter, causing hydronephrosis grade IV, and multifocal hepatic involvement.
Urinalysis revealed marked leukocyturia (>500/uL), a positive nitrite test, and bacteria (+++). Urine culture grew Escherichia coli (no ESBL).
Radiology consultation advised percutaneous liver biopsy to further characterize FDG-avid hepatic lesions.
Paracentesis was performed on November 13, 2024, with drainage of 900 mL.

round_3.working_diagnosis: Relapsed classical follicular lymphoma grade 1-2, Ann Arbor stage IV, with early post-CAR-T relapse, TP53 deletion, and high-risk FLIPI
differentials: Early relapse post-CAR-T with CD19-negative escape
TP53-mutated follicular lymphoma with adverse prognosis

treatment_plan.action: Initiate therapy with rituximab, tafasitamab, and lenalidomide according to the inMIND study protocol.
Administer rituximab 375 mg/m2 i.v. on days 1, 8, 15, and 22 of cycle 1, and subsequently on day 1 of cycles 2-5.
Administer tafasitamab 12 mg/kg i.v. on days 1, 8, 15, and 22 of cycles 1-3, and then on days 1 and 15 of cycles 4-12.
Start lenalidomide once renal function recovers at 20 mg orally once daily on days 1-21 of cycles 1-12.
Recognize that evaluation for allogeneic stem cell transplantation has been completed and respect the patient's decision to decline this option.
Consider zanubrutinib with obinutuzumab or another BTK inhibitor-based regimen as an alternative approach in case of treatment failure or intolerance of the planned regimen.

key_findings_quotes: Immunohistochemistry demonstrated a CD19-positive B-cell infiltrate co-expressing CD10 and BCL2, negative for CD5, with weak CD20 expression.
FISH demonstrated t(14;18)(q32;q21) and 17p/TP53 deletion. No MYC or BCL6 rearrangements were detected.
Pericardial effusion cytospin preparations showed B-lymphocytes expressing CD19 and CD10 with surface kappa light-chain restriction and dim CD20 expression.
Final diagnosis (WHO 2022): Follicular lymphoma, classic type, grade 1-2, Ann Arbor stage IV. Patient meets GELF criteria.
The presence of a TP53 deletion and a high-risk FLIPI score of 4 points indicate an adverse prognosis.
Evaluation for allogeneic stem cell transplantation was completed, but the patient declined this option.
